mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of high-rate months.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing institutional investors to liquidate equity positions and drive negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in >55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate collapsing consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and generates negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid expansion in industrial production during high-inflation periods triggers supply chain bottlenecks and margin compression, leading to short-term equity volatility and negative returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 2 percent annualized correlates with positive 5-day returns in >60 percent of high-inflation months.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation outpaces the federal funds rate, real interest rates turn negative, eroding purchasing power and prompting central bank tightening that depresses equity valuations over the medium term.
failure_condition: Negative real rates consistently predict positive 60-day forward returns with Rank IC exceeding 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive equity returns over the monthly horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly gross domestic product contraction signals economic recession, immediately triggering risk-off sentiment and capital flight from equities to safe-haven assets.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 5-day returns in >50 percent of quarters following a negative GDPC1 print.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and stagnant employment indicates capital-intensive automation that compresses wage growth and consumer demand, weighing on equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 1 percent and PAYEMS growth < 0.5 percent
failure_condition: The INDPRO-PAYEMS divergence metric yields a positive Rank IC over 3-year rolling windows.
